items.title: Claude's voice mode graduates from Haiku-only to Opus and Sonnet
items.summary: On July 23, Anthropic upgraded Claude's voice mode so it defaults to whatever model you were last using in text chat — including Opus and Sonnet, not just the lightweight Haiku it previously routed every voice query through. Voice mode now also supports cross-app automation with Gmail, Google Calendar, Slack, Canva, and Notion, and covers ten languages including French. Free users still get Haiku and single-app access; paid plans unlock the bigger models and multi-app features.
items.whyItMatters: Anthropic just validated the exact tradeoff Romain/Anna already navigate: routing voice to a small fast model for latency versus a bigger model for reasoning depth, with user-controlled mid-conversation switching as the resolution worth copying.

items.title: Open speech-recognition models have converged to under 1 WER point apart
items.summary: A July 23 MarkTechPost roundup compares the current crop of open ASR models — Cohere Transcribe, IBM Granite Speech 4.1, ARK-ASR-3B, MOSS-Transcribe-preview-2B, and Qwen3-ASR-0.6B — noting the top of the Hugging Face Open ASR Leaderboard is now separated by under one word-error-rate point. With quality this close, the piece argues the real decision has shifted to license, language coverage, streaming support, and cost per audio-hour.
items.whyItMatters: A 2B open-weight ASR model beating what closed APIs charged 18 months ago is a live option for a cheaper or self-hosted STT leg feeding Romain/Anna, if French coverage and streaming latency hold up under testing.

items.title: OpenAI's Presence platform bakes simulation testing into voice-agent deployment
items.summary: OpenAI launched Presence on July 22, an enterprise platform for deploying voice and chat agents that bundles policy/permission guardrails, escalation rules to route to humans, and pre-deployment simulations against edge cases before an agent goes live. OpenAI says its own English-language support line built on Presence now resolves about 75% of inbound issues after a few weeks, and cites BBVA Mexico and SoftBank Corp as early customers running agents in Spanish and Japanese.
items.whyItMatters: The pre-deployment simulation-against-edge-cases pattern, testing a voice agent against tricky scenarios before shipping, is a workflow worth borrowing for validating Romain/Anna's conversation handling before a French learner ever hits it.

items.title: White House accuses Moonshot AI of covertly distilling Anthropic's Fable into Kimi K3
items.summary: On July 22, White House OSTP director Michael Kratsios publicly accused Moonshot AI of large-scale, covert distillation of Anthropic's Fable model to build Kimi K3 — the first time a senior US official has named a specific Chinese lab copying a specific American model this way. Kratsios also alleged Moonshot accessed export-controlled Nvidia GB300 chips in Thailand; Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent followed with a warning that sanctions and Entity List designations are on the table for large-scale model distillation.
items.whyItMatters: A geopolitical fight over whether a leading open-weight model (Kimi K3, a candidate cheap-fallback LLM) was built by copying Claude is worth tracking, since export-control fallout could affect access to it or similar open models.
